In the tense silence that followed, a single, deliberate nod from across the floor confirmed that their secret concession on the Jovian shipping corridors had been accepted, sparing the family from ruin at the eleventh hour.

prose: The quartz-sheathed viewport of {given_name} {surname}’s office framed a horizon of iron-rusted silt and the sharp, artificial glare of high-altitude mirrors warming the southern poles. Below, the domed networks of the early cisterns glowed like drops of cold mercury against the red-brick wastes, while the twin notches of Phobos and Deimos scratched thin white lines across the violet-black vault. Between the glass and the desk, the holo-grid hummed with amber light, its delicate lines of light tracing the orbital paths of sixty thousand souls whose lives were reduced to tiny, flickering coordinates of pale sapphire.

Across the dark, silica-veined table sat the rival industrialist, whose eyes caught the blue luminescence of the display like chips of polished obsidian. Every micro-flicker of the planetary schematic was mirrored in those pupils—the gossamer threads of freight lines, the cold-point designations of the Kuiper Belt reserves, the raw geometry of a system being carved up like a landed whale. This map was more than an industrial ledger; it was the ink with which the {family_name} name would either write its eternal reign or be drowned under a wave of corporate consensus.

Looking at those fragile, glass-sheathed cradles clinging to the barren basalt, {given_name} saw not raw assets, but the terrifying fragility of human breath, demanding a hand that would guide rather than exploit.

choice.text: Present a comprehensive plan for regulated, ethical interstellar expansion, prioritizing sustainable growth and diplomatic parity.

prose: The raw, blinding glitter of the outer-rim projections on the display sparked a sharper fire in {given_name}'s eyes—a vision of absolute, unassailable dominance carved from the ice and iron of the deep dark.

choice.text: Propose an aggressive strategy for rapid resource acquisition and accelerated exoplanet colonization, securing dominance early.

decision.prompt: The future of human civilization beyond Earth rests on this decision. How will the {family_name} line shape humanity's first steps into the interstellar void?

options.text: Champion the establishment of an Interstellar Republic, founded on democratic principles and equitable resource sharing.
Advocate for a confederation of independent star-states, each charting its own course, with the {family_name} line acting as a central diplomatic arbiter.
Secure vast swathes of new territory for direct {family_name} oversight, establishing a hereditary claim over key resource-rich systems.

prose: The replicated polenta e uccelli, extruded by the high-pressure nozzles of the orbital galleys, left a heavy, iron-rich grease upon {given_name} {surname}’s tongue—a coarse, scalding smear of cornmeal and reconstituted wild game that tasted intensely, almost violently, of the Lombardy hills. It was a phantom flavor, thick with salt and the resinous bitterness of dried rosemary, fighting against the sterile, recycled wash of the station's atmosphere. Every mouthful carried the cold, mineral flateness of the scrubbed water supply, a reminder that their ancestors’ simple hearth-fare had been translated into an equation of proteins and lipids, consumed while looking down upon the atmosphere that once grew the grain.

The accompanying vintage, a synthetic Nebbiolo aged in the slow, spinning sickness of the low-gravity vaults, offered no soft comfort; it was sharp and astringent, leaving a dry, metallic coat of tannin along the teeth. It tasted of copper pipes and artificial oak-galls, a wine born without sun or soil, yet it carried a dark, concentrated weight that burned clean down the throat. As the bitter dregs settled, they left a stubborn, iron-dry heat in {given_name}’s mouth—a taste of survival sharpened by high-vacuum grease, the sharp, salt-bitten flavor of a dynasty that had learned to feed its children on stardust and boiled oil.
The residual acid of the synthetic vintage, sharp and lingering like a copper wire pressed to the gums, was the exact flavor of their new diplomacy—bitter, calculated, and vast.

choice.text: Reflect on the expanded political reach and the new diplomatic channels forged, envisioning further influence.

prose: Beneath the chemical sting of the artificial grape lay the unmistakable, heavy fat of the lard and corn—the ancient, stubborn starch that had sustained the {family_name} name through mud and vacuum alike.

choice.text: Consider the profound legacy passed to the next generation, a true foothold for the {family_name} line beyond Earth.

decision.prompt: The cold, iron-rich grease of the replicated game birds still coats your teeth, a reminder of the vast gulf between this sterile habitat and the warm soil of our ancestors. {given_name}, as the air recyclers hiss their synthetic breath into the chamber, how will you anchor the {family_name} name before our bloodlines grow as artificial as the food we eat?

options.text: I will swallow my distaste and sign the marriage contracts with the orbital logistics director. Let us breed heirs who are half-steel and half-Lombard, ensuring the {surname} name holds the keys to the oxygen valves.

There will be no political marriages of convenience. I shall adopt my late sister’s child, raising them on the old stories and the true, unadulterated traditions of the family, even if our branches narrow to a single, stubborn twig.

Let the line end with me. I will liquidate our orbital estates and fund the deep-space probe initiative, scattering the {surname} archives to the galactic wind so that our history outlives our biological decay.
